examTitle: CEFR B2 Mock Reading and Use of English - Environment & Climate

parts.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
parts.content: The impact of human activity on the environment is a pressing concern. Rising greenhouse gas emissions are (1) _______ our planet's climate at an alarming rate. Deforestation, pollution, and climate change are all (2) _______ effects of human activity. The consequences of these actions will be (3) _______ for generations to come. It is (4) _______ for us to take immediate action to mitigate the effects of climate change. We must (5) _______ our reliance on fossil fuels and transition to renewable energy sources. This will not only (6) _______ the environment but also create new job opportunities. However, (7) _______ the benefits of this transition, there are still many challenges to overcome.

questions.question: Gap 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'worsening' because the passage states that rising greenhouse gas emissions are having a negative impact on the planet's climate. The other options do not accurately convey this meaning.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice cloze questions, pay close attention to the context of the sentence and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 2
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'long-term' because the passage states that deforestation, pollution, and climate change are all effects of human activity that will have consequences for generations to come.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice cloze questions, be careful to read the sentence carefully and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 3
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'negative' because the passage states that the consequences of human activity will be severe.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice cloze questions, pay attention to the tone of the sentence and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 4
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'possible' because the passage states that it is necessary for us to take immediate action to mitigate the effects of climate change.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice cloze questions, be careful to read the sentence carefully and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 5
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'reduce' because the passage states that we must reduce our reliance on fossil fuels.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice cloze questions, pay attention to the verb tense and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 6
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'help' because the passage states that transitioning to renewable energy sources will create new job opportunities.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice cloze questions, be careful to read the sentence carefully and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 7
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'despite' because the passage states that despite the benefits of transitioning to renewable energy sources, there are still many challenges to overcome.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice cloze questions, pay attention to the conjunction used in the sentence and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

parts.title: Part 2: Open Cloze on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
parts.content: The (1) _______ of plastic in our oceans is a major environmental concern. It is estimated that over 8 million tons of plastic waste enter the ocean (2) _______ year. This has devastating effects on marine life, including (3) _______ of coral reefs and (4) _______ of fish populations. The use of single-use plastics is a significant contributor to this problem. It is (5) _______ for individuals and businesses to reduce their use of single-use plastics and switch to reusable alternatives.

questions.question: Gap 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'level' because the passage states that the level of plastic in our oceans is a major environmental concern.
questions.tips: For open cloze questions, pay attention to the context of the sentence and choose the word that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 2
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'each' because the passage states that over 8 million tons of plastic waste enter the ocean each year.
questions.tips: For open cloze questions, be careful to read the sentence carefully and choose the word that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 3
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'destruction' because the passage states that the effects of plastic waste on coral reefs include destruction of coral reefs.
questions.tips: For open cloze questions, pay attention to the verb used in the sentence and choose the word that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 4
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'depletion' because the passage states that the effects of plastic waste on fish populations include depletion of fish populations.
questions.tips: For open cloze questions, be careful to read the sentence carefully and choose the word that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Gap 5
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'essential' because the passage states that it is essential for individuals and businesses to reduce their use of single-use plastics.
questions.tips: For open cloze questions, pay attention to the adverb used in the sentence and choose the word that best fits the meaning.

parts.title: Part 3: Word Formation on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
parts.content: The government has implemented policies to reduce (1) _______ (EMISSION) from vehicles. This includes increasing taxes on fuel and providing incentives for people to use public transport. The city is also investing in (2) _______ (INNOVATION) to reduce energy consumption in buildings. This includes installing solar panels and upgrading insulation. The aim is to make the city (3) _______ (SUSTAINABLE) and reduce its carbon footprint.

questions.question: EMISSION
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'emissions' because the passage states that the government has implemented policies to reduce emissions from vehicles.
questions.tips: For word formation questions, read the sentence carefully and choose the word that forms a logical connection with the base word.

questions.question: INNOVATION
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'innovations' because the passage states that the city is investing in innovations to reduce energy consumption in buildings.
questions.tips: For word formation questions, pay attention to the context of the sentence and choose the word that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: SUSTAINABLE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'sustainable' because the passage states that the aim is to make the city sustainable and reduce its carbon footprint.
questions.tips: For word formation questions, be careful to read the sentence carefully and choose the word that best fits the meaning.

parts.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformations on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between two and five words, including the word given.
parts.content: This is a major environmental issue. It has serious consequences for the planet.

questions.question: It's been a long time since I last saw her.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'since I last saw her' because it conveys the same meaning as the first sentence.
questions.tips: For key word transformations, pay attention to the meaning and structure of the sentence and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: I haven't studied for this exam.
questions.correctOption: since I haven't studied for this exam
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'since I haven't studied for this exam' because it conveys the same meaning as the first sentence.
questions.tips: For key word transformations, be careful to read the sentence carefully and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

parts.title: Part 5: Multiple-Choice Reading Comprehension on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Read the text and answer Questions 1-6. For each question, choose the correct answer A, B, C or D.
parts.content: The environmental impact of human activity is a major concern. Climate change, deforestation, and pollution are all major contributors to this problem. The effects of climate change are far-reaching and devastating. Rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions are just a few of the consequences. It is essential that we take immediate action to reduce our carbon footprint and transition to renewable energy sources. This will not only help to mitigate the effects of climate change but also create new job opportunities and improve public health.

questions.question: What does the writer suggest about the environmental impact of human activity?
options: A. It is a minor concern.
B. It is a major concern.
C. It is a temporary problem.
D. It is a natural phenomenon.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'B. It is a major concern.' because the passage states that the environmental impact of human activity is a major concern.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice reading comprehension questions, pay attention to the writer's tone and message and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: What are some of the effects of climate change?
options: A. Rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions.
B. Deforestation, pollution, and climate change.
C. Rising temperatures, more frequent droughts, and extreme weather conditions.
D. Rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and improved public health.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions.' because the passage states that rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions are just a few of the consequences of climate change.
questions.tips: For multiple-choice reading comprehension questions, be careful to read the passage carefully and choose the option that best fits the meaning.

parts.title: Part 6: Gapped Text on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Six sentences have been removed from the text. Choose from the sentences A-G the one which fits each gap. There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.
parts.content: The environmental impact of human activity is a major concern. Climate change, deforestation, and pollution are all major contributors to this problem. [Gap 1] Rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions are just a few of the consequences. It is essential that we take immediate action to reduce our carbon footprint and transition to renewable energy sources. This will not only help to mitigate the effects of climate change but also create new job opportunities and improve public health. [Gap 2] The effects of climate change are far-reaching and devastating. [Gap 3] It is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address.

questions.question: Gap 1
options: A. Rising sea levels are just one of the consequences of climate change.
B. The effects of climate change are far-reaching and devastating.
C. It is essential that we take immediate action to reduce our carbon footprint and transition to renewable energy sources.
D. The consequences of climate change are not limited to just one or two areas.
E. Climate change is a major contributor to rising sea levels.
F. It is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address.
G. The effects of climate change are not yet fully understood.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A. Rising sea levels are just one of the consequences of climate change.' because it fits the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: For gapped text questions, read the passage carefully and choose the sentence that best fits the context.

questions.question: Gap 2
options: A. It is essential that we take immediate action to reduce our carbon footprint and transition to renewable energy sources.
B. Rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions are just a few of the consequences.
C. The effects of climate change are far-reaching and devastating.
D. The consequences of climate change are not limited to just one or two areas.
E. Climate change is a major contributor to rising sea levels.
F. It is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address.
G. The effects of climate change are not yet fully understood.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'D. The consequences of climate change are not limited to just one or two areas.' because it fits the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: For gapped text questions, read the passage carefully and choose the sentence that best fits the context.

questions.question: Gap 3
options: A. Rising sea levels are just one of the consequences of climate change.
B. The effects of climate change are far-reaching and devastating.
C. It is essential that we take immediate action to reduce our carbon footprint and transition to renewable energy sources.
D. The consequences of climate change are not limited to just one or two areas.
E. Climate change is a major contributor to rising sea levels.
F. It is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address.
G. The effects of climate change are not yet fully understood.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'F. It is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address.' because it fits the context of the sentence.
questions.tips: For gapped text questions, read the passage carefully and choose the sentence that best fits the context.

parts.title: Part 7: Multiple Matching on Environmental Issues
parts.instructions: Read the texts below. For Questions 1-5, choose from the sections A-D. Each section may be chosen more than once.
parts.content: A. Climate change is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address. It is essential that we take immediate action to reduce our carbon footprint and transition to renewable energy sources. This will not only help to mitigate the effects of climate change but also create new job opportunities and improve public health. B. Rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions are just a few of the consequences of climate change. It is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address. C. The effects of climate change are far-reaching and devastating. It is essential that we take immediate action to reduce our carbon footprint and transition to renewable energy sources. D. Climate change is a major contributor to rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions.

questions.question: Which text states that climate change is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'A' because it states that climate change is a major global issue that requires a concerted effort to address.
questions.tips: For multiple matching questions, read the texts carefully and choose the section that best fits the meaning.

questions.question: Which text states that rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions are just a few of the consequences of climate change?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'B' because it states that rising sea levels, more frequent natural disasters, and extreme weather conditions are just a few of the consequences of climate change.
questions.tips: For multiple matching questions, read the texts carefully and choose the section that best fits the meaning.
